channel.title: Agentic AI on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Agentic AI on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting
item.description:
The post explains MCP tool poisoning as an attack that can turn trusted AI agents into a control plane for data loss. It describes how attackers manipulate tool descriptions to induce unauthorized actions, and outlines detection, containment, and prevention approaches.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: The durable asset is the loop you own. OpenEnv is its protocol.
item.description:
The article argues that the durable asset in agentic AI is the learning loop, not the rented model, and presents OpenEnv as the protocol for reinforcement learning environments. It positions OpenEnv around owning and standardizing the environment/evaluation loop for recursive self-improvement.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Beyond the benchmark: Advancing security at AI speed
item.description:
Microsoft Security says it has advanced its agentic vulnerability detection system, MDASH, and integrated it into workflows across Windows, Azure, and identity systems. The post focuses on applying the system in real-world security operations rather than benchmark results.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL as a knowledge source in Foundry IQ
item.description:
Azure SQL Database is now available in public preview as a first-class Knowledge Source in Azure AI Search / Foundry IQ. This lets developers and architects use authoritative SQL tables as a source for Copilot, RAG, and agentic experiences.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Discovery
item.description:
Microsoft Discovery is now generally available. It provides an enterprise platform for building and governing agentic AI workflows for research and development across scientific and engineering disciplines.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog says a year of red teaming agentic AI systems led to an updated failure-mode taxonomy. The update adds seven new failure modes, including supply chain compromise and goal hijacking, along with recommended mitigations.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: Introducing Command Line, and the new rules for builders
item.description:
Microsoft introduces a new “rules for builders” framing around agentic AI development, saying the traditional software development lifecycle is no longer sufficient. The post positions the guidance as a shift in workflows and mindset for building with AI agents.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description:
The page is an article introducing an agentic AI email bot built on AWS Bedrock AgentCore with Microsoft O365 integration. The provided excerpt is only a brief tagline and does not include implementation details.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps
